title: Branch Workflow Mistakes
intro: A developer is trying to create a feature branch, do some work, and merge it back. It's like following a recipe -- skip one step and the whole dish can go wrong. But they made a common mistake that beginners often run into. Can you spot it?

teach.title: The Feature Branch Workflow = Assembly Line
teach.body: Imagine a car factory. The main assembly line produces finished cars. When engineers want to test a new engine design, they don't experiment on the main line -- they set up a **side workstation**, build and test the engine there, and only install it on the main line once it's proven to work. That's the feature branch workflow.

teach.title: How Teams Use Branches
teach.body:
Here's the typical workflow:

1. **Create a branch** from main for your feature
2. **Make commits** on your feature branch as you work
3. **Switch back to main** when the feature is done
4. **Merge** your feature branch into main

The key rule: you must be **standing on the branch that receives the changes** when you merge.

teach.title: Merging = Accepting a Delivery
teach.body: Think of merging like receiving a delivery at your front door. The delivery person (feature branch) brings the package to your house (main branch). You have to be home (standing on main) to accept the delivery. If you go to the warehouse instead, you've got it backwards -- the package doesn't end up at your house. That's why you switch to main before merging.

teach.title: The Correct Merge Workflow
teach.code:
git checkout -b feature/payments   # Create & switch to feature branch
# ... do your work, make commits ...
git checkout main                  # Switch BACK to main
git merge feature/payments         # Merge feature INTO main
teach.annotation: The merge direction matters! You stand on main and pull the feature in -- not the other way around.

teach.body: Getting the merge workflow right is what separates tutorial-level knowledge from production-ready skills. Companies use this exact workflow hundreds of times a day -- nail it, and you can confidently join any dev team building apps with AI or otherwise.
To merge a feature into main, first switch to main with `git checkout main`, then run `git merge feature/your-branch` -- always stand on the receiving branch.

exercise.prompt: The developer wants to merge their feature branch INTO main, but they're doing it backwards. Tap the buggy line.

fixes.text: git checkout main && git merge feature/payments

exercise.explanation: When you run 'git merge main' while on the feature branch, you're merging main INTO your feature branch -- the opposite of what was intended. To merge your feature INTO main, you need to first switch to main with 'git checkout main', then run 'git merge feature/payments'. Think of it this way: you always stand on the branch that receives the changes.
